mechanism_narrative: Sustained elevation of the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals monetary tightening that increases discount rates on equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors reprice growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.33% as of 2022-04-15 indicates early tightening cycle
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment of more than 0.5% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite payroll declines, or correlation between PAYEMS momentum and 20-day SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Joint deterioration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand and consumer confidence, which when both decline more than 3% month-over-month simultaneously, predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings revisions cascade downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 3% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 667724.0M and HOUST at 1793.0K as of 2022-03-15
regime_hint: demand-side contraction with synchronized weakness across consumption and housing
failure_condition: The joint signal (both RSAFS and HOUST declining > 3% simultaneously) fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) of more than 0.4% signals supply-side weakness and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice cyclical earnings downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of instances when INDPRO declines > 0.4%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Acceleration of consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 8.0% year-over-year, combined with rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 2.0%), signals the Federal Reserve is in active tightening mode to combat inflation, which compresses equity risk premiums and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 288.663 (1982-84=100) and FEDFUNDS at 0.33% as of 2022-04-15
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when both inflation and policy rate conditions are met, or the interaction coefficient between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS is not statistically significant (p > 0.12).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) remains below 4.0% while wage growth accelerates, signaling tight labor markets that increase corporate cost pressures and reduce profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations are revised downward.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.6% and PAYEMS at 151314.0K as of 2022-04-15
regime_hint: tight labor market with wage inflation pressure
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction between low UNRATE and high PAYEMS growth does not produce statistically significant negative returns (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1.5% annualized rate, signaling economic slowdown that reduces corporate revenue growth expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as investors reprice growth multiples downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when GDPC1 growth falls below 1.5%, or the correlation between GDPC1 growth rate and 60-day SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline more than 5% month-over-month, signaling a sharp contraction in residential construction activity that reduces forward economic growth expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors anticipate earnings downgrades.
regime_hint: housing market contraction or demand shock
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances when HOUST declines > 5%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window during housing downturns.
